frameworks.name: Shepard generalization gradient
frameworks.one_line: Probability of generalization decays exponentially with psychological distance.

frameworks.name: psychological space (multidimensional scaling)
frameworks.one_line: Stimuli embedded in metric space where distance governs generalization.

frameworks.name: probabilistic geometry of natural kinds
frameworks.one_line: Derives exponential law from priors over consequential regions.

frameworks.name: evolutionary internalization
frameworks.one_line: Universal regularities evolved to be common to all sentient organisms.

core_question: Is there a universal functional form by which learned responses generalize across stimuli for any sentient organism?
key_assumptions: stimuli admit a metric psychological space
generalization probability is an invariant monotonic function of distance
consequential regions in psychological space have natural-kind structure
metric form depends on whether stimulus dimensions are integral or separable
manipulations: distance between stimuli in psychological space
relation between dimensions (integral vs separable) governing metric choice
key_findings: generalization probability decays approximately exponentially with psychological distance
one of two metrics (Euclidean or city-block) applies depending on dimensional relation
interpretations: exponential decay law is mathematically derivable from universal probabilistic-geometric principles
regularity may be evolutionarily internalized across all sentient organisms
